project: Recipe Club Hub - UX & Bug Remediation
description: Fix all 22 code-level bugs (from buglist.md) and actionable UX issues (from ux-audit.md). Prioritizes critical security/data integrity bugs first, then high-severity bugs, then UX improvements. Defers major architectural changes (mobile grid redesign, drag-and-drop, interactive grocery checkboxes) to future work.

userStories.title: Fix critical RLS security vulnerabilities
userStories.description: As a developer, I want RLS policies to properly enforce authorization so that non-admin users cannot manipulate club events and grocery caches are scoped to event access.
acceptanceCriteria: Migration adds admin role check to club event management policy: USING clause includes EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM allowed_users WHERE email = auth.jwt()->>'email' AND role = 'admin') for INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE on scheduled_events where type='club'
Migration adds event access check to event_grocery_cache policies: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE policies verify the user has access to the referenced event_id
A separate SELECT policy allows all authenticated users to read club events (type='club')
Personal event policies remain unchanged (users can manage their own events where type='personal' AND created_by = auth.uid())
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: BUG-002: The policy 'Allowed users can manage club events' in migration 20260218000004_fix_club_event_policies.sql uses FOR ALL with USING (type = 'club') WITH CHECK (type = 'club') but has no admin role check. The original admin-only policy was dropped in 20260218000002_personal_meal_events.sql:18. Fix: create a new migration that drops the current permissive policy and replaces it with (1) a SELECT policy for all authenticated users on club events, and (2) an INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE policy restricted to admin role users. BUG-009: event_grocery_cache migration (20260208000000_create_event_grocery_cache.sql) has USING (true) on all policies. Fix: add a subquery checking that the event_id belongs to an event the user can access (either a club event or a personal event they created). These are SQL-only migrations — no frontend code changes needed.

userStories.title: Fix parse-recipe edge function bugs
userStories.description: As a user, I want recipe parsing to correctly handle all file types, validate file sizes, and recover gracefully from errors so that uploading photos and PDFs works reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Media type is detected from URL extension or Content-Type header instead of hard-coded 'image/jpeg' — supports image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, image/heic, application/pdf
File size is validated before processing: Content-Length header checked, files over 10MB rejected with a clear error message
Re-parse flow (delete old ingredients + insert new) is wrapped in a database transaction via an RPC function to prevent data loss on partial failure
Malformed JSON request body is caught early with a separate try-catch; recipeId is extracted before the main processing try block so error status can always be updated
JSON extraction regex handles edge cases: validates extracted JSON with JSON.parse() and falls back to full response text if regex extraction fails
Edge function returns appropriate error responses (400 for bad input, 413 for oversized files, 500 for server errors)
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: All fixes are in supabase/functions/parse-recipe/index.ts (~393 lines). BUG-001 (line 272): media_type hard-coded to 'image/jpeg'. Fix: create a helper function that maps URL extensions (.pdf→application/pdf, .png→image/png, .webp→image/webp, .heic→image/heic) with image/jpeg as default. Also check Content-Type from fetch response headers. BUG-010 (lines 110,124): no file size validation. Fix: check Content-Length header after fetch, reject >10MB. Also use a more memory-efficient base64 encoding (Uint8Array→btoa with chunking instead of String.fromCharCode per byte). BUG-014 (lines 305-328): delete then insert recipe_ingredients without transaction. Fix: create an RPC migration like 'replace_recipe_ingredients(p_recipe_id UUID, p_ingredients JSONB)' that does DELETE+INSERT in a single transaction. Call via supabase.rpc() from the edge function. BUG-015 (line 84, 360-368): req.json() can throw before recipeId is assigned. Fix: wrap req.json() in its own try-catch, extract recipeId immediately, then proceed with main logic. BUG-020 (line 295): regex /```(?:json)?\s*([\s\S]*?)```/ can truncate on nested backticks. Fix: after extraction, validate with JSON.parse(); if invalid, try parsing the full response text.

userStories.title: Fix MealPlanPage data integrity bugs
userStories.description: As a user, I want meal plan operations to be reliable so that adding, editing, and ordering meals works correctly without data loss.
acceptanceCriteria: addItemToPlan calls in handleAddRecipeMeal loop are properly awaited (add await before each call in the for loop)
Editing a meal uses UPDATE instead of delete-then-insert: handleAddCustomMeal and handleAddRecipeMeal update existing meal_plan_items in place when editingItem is present, preserving cooked_at status
New meal_plan_items are inserted with a calculated sort_order based on the max existing sort_order for that slot (day_of_week + meal_type) plus 1
Tests updated for: awaited addItemToPlan, UPDATE-based edit flow, sort_order calculation
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on mealplan directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: All fixes in src/components/mealplan/MealPlanPage.tsx (708 lines). BUG-003 (line 306): handleAddRecipeMeal has a for loop calling addItemToPlan() without await. Add await before addItemToPlan(). Also ensure setPendingSlot(null) at line 308 only runs after the loop completes. BUG-008 (lines 277-296, 299-307): Both handleAddCustomMeal and handleAddRecipeMeal delete the old item via handleRemoveMeal(editingItem.id) then insert new. Fix: when editingItem is present, use supabase .from('meal_plan_items').update({recipe_id, ...}).eq('id', editingItem.id) instead of delete+insert. This preserves cooked_at. Only delete+insert if the recipe_id itself needs to change (custom→recipe or vice versa). BUG-018 (lines 358-368): meal_plan_items insert has no sort_order. Fix: before inserting, query max sort_order for the same plan_id+day_of_week+meal_type, then insert with sort_order = max + 1 (default to 0 if none exist). Test file: tests/unit/components/mealplan/MealPlanPage.test.tsx.

userStories.title: Fix event management logic bugs
userStories.description: As a user, I want event management to handle edge cases correctly so that ingredient counts are accurate, queries don't fail on empty data, and I can select today's date for events.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipeClubEvents.tsx guards against empty arrays before calling .in(): if eventIds.length === 0, skip the recipes query and set empty results; same for recipeIds and the notes query
Ingredient used_count increment uses an atomic RPC function (UPDATE ingredients SET used_count = used_count + 1 WHERE id = $1) instead of read-then-write pattern
Migration creates the increment_ingredient_used_count RPC function
handleRatingsComplete properly checks for errors on all Supabase calls and shows error toasts on failure
EventDetailPage date picker disabled function zeros out hours on comparison date so today is selectable: const today = new Date(); today.setHours(0,0,0,0); return date < today
Tests updated for: empty array guards, RPC call for increment, error handling, date picker today
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on events directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: BUG-004: src/components/events/RecipeClubEvents.tsx lines 83,95. When eventsData is empty, eventIds is []. The .in('event_id', []) call has undefined PostgREST behavior. Fix: add early return guards. BUG-005+BUG-006: RecipeClubEvents.tsx lines 328-365. handleRatingsComplete reads used_count then writes back — race condition, plus no error check on the read. Fix: create migration with PL/pgSQL RPC: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION increment_ingredient_used_count(p_ingredient_id UUID) RETURNS void AS $$ BEGIN UPDATE ingredients SET used_count = used_count + 1, last_used_date = now() WHERE id = p_ingredient_id; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER; Then replace the read+write with a single supabase.rpc('increment_ingredient_used_count', { p_ingredient_id: ingredientId }) call. Still need to separately update last_used_by. BUG-007: src/pages/EventDetailPage.tsx line 1715. Fix: disabled={(date) => { const today = new Date(); today.setHours(0,0,0,0); return date < today; }}.

userStories.title: Fix error handling and null safety bugs
userStories.description: As a user, I want proper error handling and feedback throughout the app so that failures are visible and state is consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: auth.ts getCurrentUser (line 60-64) destructures and logs error from profile query; fallback to session data still occurs but error is logged
UserManagement.tsx (line 108-114) checks session.session?.user.id is truthy before proceeding with invited_by; shows error toast if session is expired
EventDetailPage.tsx (line 954-965) shows a toast warning when Google Calendar sync fails instead of only console.warn
RecipeHub.tsx getIngredientColor calls are guarded against undefined ingredientName (verify existing guards at lines 123, 174 are correct)
AuthGuard.tsx useEffect has a mounted flag cleanup to prevent state updates on unmounted component; same fix applied to Index.tsx useEffect
Tests updated for all changes
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: BUG-011: src/lib/auth.ts lines 60-64. The profile .single() query doesn't destructure error. Fix: const { data: profileData, error: profileError } = await supabase... then if (profileError) console.error('Profile query failed:', profileError). BUG-012: src/components/admin/UserManagement.tsx lines 108-114. invited_by uses session.session?.user.id without null check. Fix: const userId = session.session?.user.id; if (!userId) { toast.error('Session expired'); return; }. BUG-013: src/pages/EventDetailPage.tsx lines 954-965. Calendar update failure only console.warns. Fix: add toast.error('Calendar sync failed. The event date was updated but your Google Calendar may be out of sync.'). BUG-016: src/components/recipes/RecipeHub.tsx lines 123, 174. Current code already has ternary guard: ingredientName ? getIngredientColor(ingredientName) : undefined. Verify this is correct and no other unguarded calls exist. BUG-017: src/components/auth/AuthGuard.tsx (43 lines). No cleanup in useEffect. Fix: add let mounted = true at top of effect, check if (mounted) before setIsAuthed/setIsChecking, return () => { mounted = false } cleanup. Apply same pattern to src/pages/Index.tsx lines 11-21.

userStories.title: Fix low-severity code quality issues
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clean, type-safe code with no assertion risks so that edge cases don't cause runtime errors.
acceptanceCriteria: AddMealDialog.tsx line 117: fileInputRef.current! replaced with optional chaining: if (fileInputRef.current) fileInputRef.current.value = ''
AddMealDialog.tsx line 160-162: clearTimeout(debounceRef.current!) replaced with clearTimeout(debounceRef.current ?? undefined) or guard with if statement
Upload logic extracted into a shared utility function in src/lib/upload.ts that both EventDetailPage and PersonalMealDetailPage can use (same bucket, same validation, same public URL retrieval)
Tests added for the shared upload utility
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: BUG-019: src/components/mealplan/AddMealDialog.tsx line 117. In the finally block of handleFileUpload: fileInputRef.current!.value = ''. If dialog unmounts during upload, this crashes. Fix: use optional chaining. BUG-022: AddMealDialog.tsx lines 160-162. The useEffect cleanup uses clearTimeout(debounceRef.current!). While clearTimeout(undefined) is harmless in practice, the ! assertion is technically incorrect. Fix: remove the ! or add a guard. BUG-021: PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx lines 315-320 and EventDetailPage.tsx lines 608-609 both upload to recipe-images bucket with slightly different patterns. Fix: extract a shared uploadRecipeFile(file: File): Promise<string> utility in src/lib/upload.ts that handles bucket upload, public URL retrieval, and error handling. Both pages should import and use this utility. Add tests in tests/unit/lib/upload.test.ts.

userStories.title: Improve auth error feedback and session handling
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback when sign-in fails or my session expires so that I understand what happened and what to do next.
acceptanceCriteria: GoogleSignIn.tsx shows a toast.error on sign-in failure (both Google OAuth and email auth) instead of only logging to console
AuthGuard.tsx shows a toast message like 'Your session has expired. Please sign in again.' before redirecting to / when session is no longer valid
Dev mode password placeholder text changed from 'any password' to something accurate like 'Password'
Tests updated for sign-in error toast, session expiry message, placeholder text
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 1 findings. GoogleSignIn.tsx:23-24 — error is caught and console.error'd but no toast. Fix: add toast.error('Sign in failed. Please try again.') in the catch block. For Google OAuth, also handle the case where the OAuth popup is closed without completing (don't show error for user-initiated cancel). AuthGuard.tsx:20-25 — when session check fails or session is expired, user sees spinner then redirect with no explanation. Fix: before navigate('/'), show toast.info('Your session has expired. Please sign in again.'). GoogleSignIn.tsx:54 — the email form password placeholder says 'any password'. Fix: change to 'Password'. Note: the auth tests use vi.resetModules() + vi.doMock() + dynamic import() pattern — see activity.md Codebase Patterns.

userStories.title: Improve Home tab non-admin experience
userStories.description: As a non-admin user, I want useful content and navigation on the Home tab so that I'm not stuck on a dead-end screen.
acceptanceCriteria: HomeSection.tsx non-admin empty state (no active event) includes a button/link that navigates to the Recipes tab
CountdownCard.tsx 'It's Time!' state includes guidance text like 'Head to the event to see recipes and start cooking!' with a link to the event detail page
IngredientBank.tsx shows a message when the bank reaches capacity (e.g., 'Bank is full! Spin the wheel to use an ingredient.')
Time format is consistent: CountdownCard uses the same AM/PM casing as IngredientWheel
Tests updated for: non-admin CTA button, countdown guidance, bank full message, time format
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 3 findings. HomeSection.tsx (89 lines): lines 71-83 render a Card saying 'No Event Scheduled' with text suggesting to browse Recipes tab but no button. Fix: add a Button with onClick={() => navigate('/dashboard/recipes')} or use the tab switching mechanism. The component receives a setActiveTab prop or can use react-router navigation. CountdownCard.tsx: when countdown reaches zero, shows pulsing 'It's Time!' text (around the countdown display logic). Fix: below the pulsing text, add a small paragraph with a link to the event detail: 'Head to the event for recipes and cooking!' linked to /events/{eventId}. The eventId should be available from the activeEvent prop. IngredientBank.tsx: the add section conditionally renders based on ingredient count vs MIN_INGREDIENTS_TO_SPIN. When hidden, no explanation is shown. Fix: when count >= MIN_INGREDIENTS_TO_SPIN, show a Badge or small text like 'Bank full — spin the wheel!'. Time format: CountdownCard uses 'h:mm A' (7:00 PM) while IngredientWheel uses lowercase (7:00 pm). Standardize on uppercase AM/PM.

userStories.title: Improve Recipe Hub search and empty states
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search recipes by ingredient name and see accurate empty states so that I can find recipes easily and understand what actions are available.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipeHub.tsx search filter (lines 312-325) also matches against recipe.ingredientName (the ingredient associated with the recipe's event)
Personal recipes empty state text no longer mentions 'save a club recipe' (feature was removed); says something like 'No personal recipes yet. Add one using the button above!'
Club and Personal tab labels show recipe counts: 'Club (N)' and 'My Recipes (N)'
Sort dropdown added with options: Newest First (default), Alphabetical (A-Z), Highest Rated
Tests updated for: ingredient name search, empty state text, recipe counts, sort options
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on recipes directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 6 findings. RecipeHub.tsx (446 lines). Search (lines 312-325): filteredRecipes filters by name and notes only. Fix: add || recipe.ingredientName?.toLowerCase().includes(searchTerm.toLowerCase()) to the matchesSearch condition. The ingredientName field is already loaded from the ingredients join. Empty state: the personal tab empty state text is outdated (mentions saving club recipes, but SaveRecipeButton was removed in US-002). Fix: update the text. Recipe counts: the sub-tab buttons show 'Club Recipes' and 'My Recipes'. Fix: show counts like 'Club Recipes (12)' and 'My Recipes (3)' using recipes.length filtered by type. Sort: add a Select dropdown next to the search bar with options. Implement sorting on the filteredRecipes array: by created_at desc (newest), by name asc (alphabetical), by average rating desc (highest rated). The rating data is available in recipe.notes which contain ratings.

userStories.title: Add personal recipe edit and delete in Recipe Hub
userStories.description: As a user, I want to edit or delete my personal recipes directly from the Recipe Hub so that I can manage my recipe collection without navigating elsewhere.
acceptanceCriteria: RecipeCard.tsx shows Edit and Delete icon buttons for personal recipes (isPersonal === true)
Edit button opens an inline edit form or dialog to update recipe name and URL
Delete button shows a confirmation AlertDialog before deleting the recipe and its associated data
RecipeHub.tsx passes onEdit and onDelete callbacks to RecipeCard for personal recipes
After edit/delete, the recipe list refreshes to reflect changes
Tests added for: edit/delete buttons visibility, edit flow, delete confirmation, callback handling
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on recipes directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 6: 'No way to edit or delete personal recipes from Recipe Hub.' RecipeCard.tsx (248 lines) has isPersonal prop (line 38) but no edit/delete UI. Fix: (1) Add onEdit?: (recipe: RecipeWithNotes) => void and onDelete?: (recipeId: string) => void props to RecipeCard. (2) When isPersonal && onEdit && onDelete, render small icon buttons (Pencil, Trash2 from lucide-react) in the card header next to the Personal badge. (3) In RecipeHub.tsx, add handleEditPersonalRecipe that opens a dialog (reuse the pattern from AddPersonalRecipeDialog — a Dialog with name + URL fields, pre-filled with current values, that calls supabase.from('recipes').update({name, url}).eq('id', recipeId)). (4) Add handleDeletePersonalRecipe that shows an AlertDialog confirmation then calls supabase.from('recipes').delete().eq('id', recipeId) and refreshes the list. (5) Pass these as onEdit/onDelete to RecipeCard only on the personal tab. RecipeCard tests: tests/unit/components/recipes/RecipeCard.test.tsx. RecipeHub tests: tests/unit/components/recipes/RecipeHub.test.tsx.

userStories.title: Improve meal plan slot labels and action UX
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear labels on meal plan action buttons and helpful feedback so that I can use the meal plan confidently on any device.
acceptanceCriteria: MealPlanSlot.tsx action buttons have visible text labels alongside icons (e.g., 'View', 'Undo', 'Done', 'Add') or at minimum aria-labels for accessibility
MealPlanPage.tsx 'Undo Cook' action shows a brief confirmation dialog before resetting cooked_at
Groceries tab empty state distinguishes between 'no meals planned' and 'meals planned but no recipes with ingredients': shows appropriate message for each case
MealPlanSlot date labels use readable font size (at least text-sm instead of text-xs)
Tests updated for: button labels/aria-labels, undo confirmation, empty state messages, date label styling
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on mealplan directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 7 findings. MealPlanSlot.tsx (134 lines): lines 91-129 have 4 icon-only buttons (ChefHat, RotateCcw, Check, Plus) with only title attributes. Fix: add short text labels next to icons. On mobile, these should still be compact — consider using sr-only text + aria-label for the tightest spaces, or abbreviations ('View', 'Undo', 'Cook', '+'). Also add aria-label attributes to all icon buttons. Undo Cook (MealPlanPage.tsx): handleUncook currently resets cooked_at immediately. Fix: add a state like uncookConfirmSlot and show an AlertDialog: 'This will mark the meal as uncooked. Ratings will be preserved. Continue?'. Grocery empty state (MealPlanPage.tsx lines 228-236): currently says 'Add meals to your plan...' even when custom meals exist but have no recipe_id/ingredients. Fix: check if there are meals in the current week but none have recipe_ids — show 'Your planned meals don't have linked recipes. Add a recipe URL to see ingredients here.' vs 'No meals planned this week. Add meals to see a grocery list.'. MealPlanGrid.tsx date labels: lines 31-36 use text-xs. Change to text-sm.

userStories.title: Improve event detail parse flow UX
userStories.description: As a user, I want the recipe parsing flow to be interruptible and provide clear recovery options so that I'm never stuck waiting on a slow parse.
acceptanceCriteria: EventDetailPage.tsx add-recipe dialog can be dismissed during parsing state with a warning: 'Parsing is in progress. Close anyway? The recipe will be saved but may not have parsed ingredients.'
Parse failure state provides clearer messaging: explains that the recipe was saved successfully but ingredient extraction failed, and offers to try again or keep as-is
Edit recipe dialog allows saving without a URL (URL field is optional, matching the add flow where file uploads don't require a separate URL)
Tests updated for: dismissible parse dialog, parse failure messaging, edit without URL
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on events directory (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 5 findings. EventDetailPage.tsx (1799 lines). Parse dialog dismissibility (line 1371): onOpenChange checks parseStatus !== 'parsing' and blocks close. Fix: allow close during parsing but show a warning via window.confirm() or a nested AlertDialog: 'Parsing is in progress. The recipe has been saved. Close anyway?'. If confirmed, close dialog and let parsing continue in background. Parse failure recovery (lines 1433-1448): current UX shows 'Keep Recipe Anyway' and 'Try Different URL'. Fix: reword to clarify the recipe IS saved — 'Your recipe has been saved! However, we couldn't extract ingredients automatically. You can: [Try parsing again] [Continue without ingredients]'. Remove the confusing 'Try Different URL' which deletes and re-adds. Edit recipe URL validation (line 1585): disabled={!isValidUrl(editRecipeUrl)} makes URL required. Fix: make URL optional — disabled={isEditingRecipe || !editRecipeName.trim()} (remove URL validation from disabled condition).

userStories.title: Consolidate pantry components and improve UX
userStories.description: As a user, I want consistent pantry management across all views with proper feedback so that managing pantry items is intuitive and reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Shared PantryContent component extracted from PantryDialog/PantrySection, used by both with variant prop (dialog vs card wrapper)
PantryDialog.tsx and PantrySection.tsx refactored to use PantryContent internally
Delete pantry item shows a brief confirmation (e.g., AlertDialog: 'Remove [item] from pantry?')
Adding a pantry item shows a success toast ('Added [item] to pantry')
Error messages are specific instead of generic ('Failed to add item. It may already exist.' becomes actual error-specific messages)
Meals Groceries tab has a small 'Manage Pantry' link/button that opens PantryDialog
Pantry exclusion feedback in GroceryListSection is more prominent (e.g., a dismissible banner instead of small text)
Tests updated for all changes
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Sections 8, 10. PantryDialog.tsx (~156 lines) and PantrySection.tsx (~156 lines) are near-identical with minor wrapper differences (Dialog vs Card). Fix: extract a PantryContent component (src/components/pantry/PantryContent.tsx) with the shared add/remove logic and item list rendering. PantryDialog wraps it in a Dialog, PantrySection wraps it in a Card. Delete confirmation: currently trash icon immediately deletes. Fix: add AlertDialog around the delete action. Success feedback: addItem currently clears input without toast. Fix: add toast.success(`Added '${itemName}' to pantry`). Error specificity: catch block shows generic message. Fix: check error.code — if '23505' (unique violation), say 'This item is already in your pantry'; otherwise show the actual error message. Pantry link in Meals: in MealPlanPage.tsx groceries view, add a small Button that opens PantryDialog. Import PantryDialog and add it to the groceries view header. GroceryListSection pantry exclusion text (line 245): change from small muted text to a visible info banner with an icon.

userStories.title: Improve upload UX and consistency
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear upload buttons with progress feedback and consistent parsing behavior so that uploading recipe photos works the same way everywhere.
acceptanceCriteria: Upload buttons in EventDetailPage and AddMealDialog have visible text labels ('Upload Photo/PDF' or similar) alongside the Upload icon
Upload progress shows the file name being uploaded and a determinate or textual progress indicator
PersonalMealDetailPage upload triggers parse-recipe after upload (matching EventDetailPage behavior)
File upload auto-fill behavior is consistent: both EventDetailPage and AddMealDialog auto-fill the recipe name from filename when name field is empty
Tests updated for: upload button labels, progress display, PersonalMealDetailPage parse trigger, name auto-fill
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 11 findings. Upload buttons: EventDetailPage.tsx lines 1474-1486 and AddMealDialog.tsx lines 226-240 both show only an Upload icon. Fix: add text label 'Upload' or 'Photo/PDF' next to the icon. For mobile, at minimum add an aria-label. Upload progress: currently shows only a Loader2 spinner. Fix: show the filename being uploaded (truncated if long) and optionally a 'Uploading...' text. Since Supabase storage doesn't provide upload progress events, a determinate progress bar isn't feasible — use an indeterminate bar with filename. PersonalMealDetailPage parse: PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx lines 341-372 uploads and saves but doesn't invoke parse-recipe. Fix: after successful upload+save in handleAddRecipe, invoke supabase.functions.invoke('parse-recipe', { body: { recipeId, recipeUrl, recipeName } }) — same pattern as EventDetailPage and MealPlanPage. Name auto-fill: EventDetailPage fills URL but not name from filename. AddMealDialog fills name from filename. Fix: make both consistent by also auto-filling name in EventDetailPage when the name/title field is empty.

userStories.title: Improve Dashboard navigation and mobile tab UX
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear navigation and informative labels so that I can move between views confidently on any device.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard tab bar shows short text labels below icons on mobile (e.g., 'Home', 'Events', 'Recipes', 'Meals')
Detail pages (EventDetailPage, PersonalMealDetailPage) back buttons always navigate to the correct Dashboard tab regardless of how the user arrived
Cancel event confirmation dialog mentions all affected data: associated recipes, meal plan references, Google Calendar event, and ratings
Header event/recipe counts clarified: show 'N Club Events' and 'N Club Recipes' or add tooltip explaining these are community-wide counts
Tests updated for: mobile tab labels, back navigation, cancel event warning, count labels
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 2 findings. Dashboard.tsx (lines 280-295): tab bar shows only icons on mobile. Fix: add short text labels (text-xs) below each icon. Use a flex-col layout for each tab button on small screens. Detail page back buttons: EventDetailPage.tsx line 1156 navigates to /dashboard/events and PersonalMealDetailPage.tsx line 575 navigates to /dashboard/meals. These are fine for normal navigation but if user arrived via deep link, they might not know about the dashboard. Fix: ensure back button always uses navigate(-1) with a fallback to the dashboard tab. Cancel event: RecipeClubEvents.tsx lines 385-413 and EventDetailPage.tsx lines 978-1017 show cancel confirmation. Fix: enhance the warning message to mention all cascading effects: 'This will permanently delete the event and all associated recipes, notes, ratings, meal plan references, and Google Calendar event. This cannot be undone.' Header counts: Dashboard.tsx lines 136-155 show 'N Events' and 'N Recipes'. Fix: change labels to 'N Club Events' and 'N Club Recipes' to clarify these are community-wide.

userStories.title: Accessibility improvements
userStories.description: As a user with accessibility needs, I want proper labels, sufficient touch targets, and text alternatives so that I can use the app with assistive technology.
acceptanceCriteria: All icon-only buttons throughout the app have aria-label attributes describing their action
Color-only status indicators (green for cooked) also have text alternatives (e.g., 'Cooked' badge or sr-only text)
Touch targets for interactive elements are at least 44x44px (increase h-3 w-3 icon buttons to minimum h-8 w-8 with padding)
Photo thumbnails in RecipeCard and EventRecipesTab have descriptive alt text
Recipe note photos have alt text describing content (e.g., 'Photo for [recipe name]')
Tests updated for: aria-labels, text alternatives, alt text attributes
Typecheck passes (npm run build)
Tests pass with 100% coverage on all required directories (npm run test:coverage)
userStories.notes: UX Audit Section 12 Cross-Cutting. This story touches many components with small changes. Key files: MealPlanSlot.tsx (icon buttons lines 91-129 — most already have title attributes, add aria-label too), RecipeCard.tsx (photo thumbnails need alt text), EventRecipesTab.tsx (note photos h-20 w-20 need alt text, line 251), MealPlanGrid.tsx (date labels). For cooked status: MealPlanSlot.tsx renders green background when isCooked — add a sr-only span with text 'Cooked' for screen readers. For touch targets: icon buttons using h-3 w-3 should be wrapped in a clickable area of at least h-8 w-8 (32px) or ideally h-11 w-11 (44px). Use padding/margin to achieve touch target without changing visual size. Photo alt text: use recipe name + 'photo' or note context. Systematic approach: grep for icon-only buttons across all components, ensure each has aria-label.

userStories.title: Final verification and coverage check
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all changes verified with passing builds, full test coverage, and clean lint so that the codebase is production-ready.
acceptanceCriteria: npm run build passes with no TypeScript errors
npm run test:coverage shows 100% on all required directories (events, ingredients, mealplan, recipes, lib)
npm run lint passes with no errors
IngredientWheel.tsx remains the only exception (~55% coverage acceptable)
No orphaned imports, unused variables, or dead code from the remediation changes
All new utility files (upload.ts, PantryContent.tsx) have 100% test coverage
userStories.notes: This is the final cleanup story. Run npm run test:coverage and check every file in the coverage report. Fix any gaps. Common issues after a large remediation pass: (1) new components not tested, (2) new utility functions without tests, (3) modified test expectations that don't match updated UI text, (4) unused imports from refactored components. Run npm run build to catch TypeScript errors. Run npm run lint for style. The coverage report 'Uncovered Line #s' column shows exactly what needs tests. If any coverage is below 100%, trace the uncovered lines back to the source and add appropriate tests.
